tex:
Flo has a bag of counters.
\newline

She picks a counter at random from the bag.
\newline

The probability that she picks a green counter is 0.52.
\newline

(a) 
Work out the probability that she does **not** pick a green counter.
\newline

The bag contains three colours of counters, green, pink and red.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Colour of counter & Green & Pink & Red \\
\hline
Probability & 0.52 & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
There are three times as many pink counters as red counters.
\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

Flo picks one counter from the bag at random, records the colour and replaces it in the bag.
\newline

She does this 200 times.
\newline

Calculate the expected number of times she picks a green counter.
\newline

content.content:
Work out the probability that she does \textbf{not} pick a green counter.
\newline

\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

Flo picks one counter from the bag at random, records the colour and replaces it in the bag.
\newline

She does this 200 times.
\newline

\newline
